outlook.headline: AI datacenter power delivery demand is broadening beyond initial adopters
outlook.summary: Multiple suppliers now point to stronger design activity and demand pull in AI datacenter power, from GaN power solutions to high-voltage gate drivers and power supplies. GlobalFoundries, ON Semiconductor, Allegro MicroSystems, Veeco, and Littelfuse all read positively, while AMD remains non-committal, suggesting the theme is strengthening in the supply chain even if public mention remains low and flat.

drivers.label: AI data center power-tree opportunity expanding
drivers.detail: Broader opportunity set in the full power tree is supporting demand for power-delivery content.

drivers.label: Design progress in high-voltage gate drivers and power supplies
drivers.detail: Growing design momentum indicates customers are moving further along the adoption curve.

drivers.label: Strategic design win in data center power solutions
drivers.detail: A named win signals continued product traction and near-term demand momentum.

drivers.label: AI datacenter power efficiency driving GaN solutions
drivers.detail: Efficiency needs are a durable long-term tailwind for GaN-based power products.

drivers.label: Data center power named as supported application
drivers.detail: Supporting this application points to solid demand tied to ongoing buildouts.

risks: Mention trend is still very low and flat, so broader market enthusiasm has not clearly accelerated yet.
AMD gave only a power-visibility comment, offering little confirmation of end-demand strength.
Design wins need to convert into production ramps; any AI buildout timing delays could push out revenue recognition.
